query: Big Tech M&A activity 2026 pace, record $10B+ deals, and antitrust environment for large tech acquisitions in 2026
answer:
I've completed the research on Big Tech M&A activity in 2026. Here's a summary of the key findings:

**M&A Pace**: 2026 follows a record 2025 ($4.9T global, +40% YoY) with a "fewer deals, bigger stakes" pattern — Q1 2026 value hit $1.2T+ (up 26% YoY) even as deal counts fell 17%. Forecasters project ~8% US deal-volume growth.

**Record $10B+ Deals**: Q1 2026 set a quarterly record of **22 deals above $10 billion** (following 60 such deals in all of 2025). Four of the six biggest Q1 deals were AI-race companies. Notable megadeals: Paramount/Warner Bros (~$170B), OpenAI (~$110B), plus large energy/consumer deals. Pure-tech acquisitions were more modest (TI/Silicon Labs $7.5B, onsemi/Synaptics ~$7B), with much AI value flowing through equity stakes rather than outright M&A.

**Antitrust Environment**: The second Trump administration is more deal-friendly than Biden's — accepting structural divestiture remedies and scaling back skepticism of AI startup tuck-ins — but still active (FTC challenged 9 mergers in 2025, DOJ 4). Big Tech's largest platforms (Google, Meta, Amazon, Apple, Microsoft) remain under active investigation, though enforcers have had mixed litigation success (e.g., FTC lost the Meta case). Large deals can clear on scale/efficiency grounds.

Full structured findings with 14 quantitative data points and 11 sources have been submitted.

key_facts: Global M&A reached a record $4.9 trillion in 2025, up ~40% YoY, surpassing the prior record of $4.86T set in 2021.
Q1 2026 global M&A exceeded $1.2 trillion (+26% YoY by value) even as the number of deals fell 17% YoY.
Q1 2026 set a quarterly record of 22 deals above $10 billion; four of the six biggest were AI-race companies.
In 2025, 60 deals exceeded $10 billion — the most since the 2021 peak; mega-deals (>$5B) drove 73%+ of the value increase.
Jan–Feb 2026: overall deal announcements down 22% YoY, but $1B+ megadeals up 57% (22 vs. 14).
Largest early/mid-2026 deals: Paramount/Warner Bros ~$170B, OpenAI ~$110B (equity), McCormick/Unilever food ~$65B, Devon/Coterra ~$58B, Anthropic ~$30B.
Pure-tech 2026 acquisitions were smaller: TI/Silicon Labs $7.5B, onsemi/Synaptics ~$7B, Hg/OneStream $6.4B.
Equity stake purchases (not traditional M&A) made up 29% of Q1 2026 deal volume, largely AI-driven.
EY-Parthenon (June 2026) projects ~8% US deal-volume growth for deals >$100M, 11% for corporate M&A.
Trump FTC challenged 9 mergers in 2025 (vs 5 in Biden's final year); DOJ challenged 4 (vs 1), mostly horizontal theories.
Big Tech remains a top DOJ/FTC priority; FTC lost its Meta Instagram/WhatsApp monopoly case; Google avoided Chrome/Android divestiture (Sept 2025).
Trump enforcers scaled back Biden-era skepticism of startup/AI tuck-in acquisitions but retain innovation-harm theories; large deals can clear on scale/efficiency grounds (T-Mobile/UScellular).
Big Tech (Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta) 2026 combined AI capex is guided at ~$650–725B, diverting capital from M&A.

data_points.metric: EY-Parthenon projected 2026 US deal volume growth (>$100M deals)
